messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0059",
  "title": "全链路增长策略指南",
  "page_index": 41,
  "page_range": "41"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0060",
    "title": "一套简单、可复制的盈利增长框架，助力移动应用在2026年破局盈利",
    "page_range": "41-42",
    "summary": "This parent section (Pages 41–51) presents a simple, replicable, and profitable full-link growth framework designed to help mobile applications overcome current market ceilings and achieve sustainable profitability by 2026. It synthesizes strategic shifts, operational workflows, quantitative benchmarks, and a documented case study to establish a new standard for mobile growth.\n\n**Topics & Claims:** The section addresses the limitations of short-term, performance-first marketing, asserting that widening gaps between ad spend and user scale indicate a strategic ceiling. It claims that true growth begins post-installation, that app stores are no longer exclusive entry points, and that sustainable competitive advantage relies on continuous learning, rational pacing (Scale, Pause, Reinvest), and shifting from a \"growth cycle\" to a \"value cycle.\" Retention is positioned as the new core performance metric, while performance marketing evolves into the new brand strategy.\n\n**Definitions:** Mobile growth is conceptually defined as equivalent to \"full-link growth\" (全链路增长). The framework introduces the \"Infinity Loop\" (a cyclical system replacing linear funnels), the \"unified learning engine\" (integrating UA, engagement, and remarketing), and the \"value cycle\" (an insight-driven feedback loop optimizing LTV). User Acquisition (UA) is defined as capturing attention for initial actions; Remarketing (RT) as continuous interaction to reactivate users and strengthen retention. Retention is defined as a continuous effect system rather than an isolated stage, and remarketing is characterized as R&D.\n\n**Procedures & Mechanisms:** The operational workflow details a continuous cycle: real-time performance learning → cross-channel insight application → transparent optimization during data si..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0094",
    "title": "2025 GLOBAL MOBILE GAME MARKETING TREND INSIGHTS 2025年手游热门品类营销洞察 05",
    "page_range": "51",
    "summary": "This section functions as a title/header for a report segment analyzing 2025 global mobile game marketing trends, with a specific focus on marketing insights for popular mobile game categories. It can answer high-level scoping questions regarding the report’s target year (2025), industry focus (mobile gaming), and thematic scope (marketing trends and category-specific strategies). The provided text contains no substantive definitions, procedures, claims, tables, figures, metrics, or evidence types, and therefore cannot answer detailed analytical, methodological, or data-driven questions. For retrieval purposes, this node should be treated strictly as a structural marker indicating the beginning of a category-focused marketing analysis within the broader 2025 trend report."
  }
]

message.content:
This parent section (Pages 41–51) outlines a comprehensive, replicable full-link growth framework for mobile applications, concluding with a structural header for 2025 mobile gaming marketing insights.

**Topics & Claims:** Critiques short-term, performance-first marketing, claiming that widening gaps between ad spend and user scale signal a strategic ceiling. Asserts that sustainable growth begins post-installation, app stores are no longer exclusive entry points, and competitive advantage depends on continuous learning, rational pacing (Scale, Pause, Reinvest), and shifting from a linear "growth cycle" to an insight-driven "value cycle." Claims performance marketing now functions as brand strategy, while retention replaces traditional metrics as the core performance indicator.

**Definitions & Entities:** Equates mobile growth with "full-link growth" (全链路增长). Defines the "Infinity Loop" (a cyclical system replacing linear funnels), the "unified learning engine" (integrates UA, engagement, and remarketing), and the "value cycle" (an insight-driven feedback loop optimizing LTV). User Acquisition (UA) is defined as capturing attention for initial actions; Remarketing (RT) as continuous interaction for reactivation and retention, characterized as an R&D function. Retention is defined as a continuous effect system rather than an isolated stage. Key entities include mobile applications, mobile games, UA, and RT.

**Procedures & Mechanisms:** Details a continuous operational workflow: real-time performance learning → cross-channel insight application → transparent optimization during data synchronization (source text truncated at this point).

**Metrics & Evidence:** Primary metrics include retention, Lifetime Value (LTV), and the ad spend-to-user scale ratio. Evidence types consist of quantitative benchmarks and a documented case study. No specific tables or figures are referenced in the provided summaries.

**Dates & Context:** Targets sustainable profitability by 2026. The section concludes on Page 51 with a structural marker introducing the "2025 Global Mobile Game Marketing Trend Insights," focusing on category-specific marketing strategies for the mobile gaming industry. Functions as a high-level scoping reference for trend analysis and category-focused strategy.

**Topics & Claims:** Critiques short-term, performance-first marketing, claiming that widening gaps between ad spend and user scale signal a strategic ceiling. Defines the "Infinity Loop" (a cyclical system replacing linear funnels), the "unified learning engine" (integrates UA, engagement, and remarketing), and the "value cycle" (an insight-driven feedback loop optimizing LTV). User Acquisition (UA) is defined as capturing attention for initial actions; Remarketing (RT) as continuous interaction for reactivation and retention, characterized as an R&D function. Retention is defined as a continuous effect system rather than an isolated stage. Key entities include mobile applications, mobile games, UA, and RT.

**Procedures & Mechanisms:** Details a continuous operational workflow: real-time performance learning → cross-channel insight application → transparent optimization during data synchronization (source text truncated at this point).

**Metrics & Evidence:** Primary metrics include retention, Lifetime Value (LTV), and the ad spend-to-user scale ratio. Evidence types consist of quantitative benchmarks and a documented case study. No specific tables or figures are referenced in the provided summaries.

**Dates & Context:** Targets sustainable profitability by 2026. The section concludes on Page 51 with a structural marker introducing the "2025 Global Mobile Game Marketing Trend Insights," focusing on category-specific marketing strategies for the mobile gaming industry. Functions as a high-level scoping reference for trend analysis and category-focused strategy.
